LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS AMERICAN L & L EDUCATION INC., JEANNE LAW, WILLIAM LAW, DELTA KING ENTERPRISES, INC., AND HENRY LAW. MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS CODE 17200 DOES NOT AUTHORIZE AN AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES. THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER DEFENDANTS' ARGUMENT MADE IN THE REPLY BRIEF BASED ON CIVIL CODE 3426.4 BECAUSE THAT ARGUMENT WAS NOT MADE IN THE MOVING PAPERS AND PLAINTIFF HAD NO OPPORTUNITY TO RESPOND TO THE ARGUMENT. AS TO COSTS, A COURT DETERMINES THE PROPRIETY OF CLAIMED COSTS ON A MOTION TO TAX OR STRIKE CLAIMED COSTS, WHICH THIS MOTION IS NOT. THE MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANTS RE-FILING SOLELY TO ARGUE FEE ENTITLEMENT UNDER CIVIL CODE SEC. 3426.4. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR #11185 (302/HK)

MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Apr-18-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Thomas of Law Offices of Richard Wahng telephone number 510-491-5021 for Plaintiff Valley International Academy.
Rocky Ortega of Law Office of Rocky Ortega telephone number 408-667-4313 appeared by telephone for Defendant American L & L of Educational Inc.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DELTA KING ENTERPRISE, INC'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES is denied. Plaintiff Valley International Academy did not allege a claim for misappropriation of trade secrets and, even if it did, Delta King has not shown that it was made in bad faith. In all events, an award of fees under Civil Code 3426.4 is discretionary and the facts and circumstances of this case do not warrant the exercise of discretion to make such an award. Order signed in open Court.

Notice Of Motion For Attorney Fees And Cost
Off Calendar Feb-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. CHRISTOPHER THOMAS, ESQ. of Law Offices of Richard Wahng, Tel No. 510-491-5021 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. ROCKY ORTEGA, ESQ. of Law Office of Rocky Ortega, Tel No. 408-667-4313 appearing telephonically for Defendants American L & L Education Inc., Jeanne Law, William Law, Delta King Enterprises, Inc., and Henry Law.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with a modification, and orders as follows: Defendants American L & L Education Inc., Jeanne Law, William Law, Delta King Enterprises, Inc., and Henry Law. Motion for Attorney's Fees and Costs is denied in its entirety. Business and Professions Code 17200 does not authorize an award of attorney's fees. The court did not consider defendants' argument made in the reply brief based on Civil Code 3426.4 because that argument was not made in the moving papers and plaintiff had no opportunity to respond to the argument. As to costs, a court determines the propriety of claimed costs on a motion to tax or strike claimed costs, which this motion is not. The motion is denied without prejudice to defendants re-filing solely to argue fee entitlement under Civil Code Sec. 3426.4. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185 (302/HK)
